title: trailer anytime soon
wikitext:
[]: trailer anytime soon??             Quote Post by Annihilator » Fri Jun 15, 2007 9:42 pm Theres only a few months left until the movie, dose anyone know when there going to show us a new trailer? all we have is that old one that only shows text.

[]:             Quote Post by reagen » Sat Jun 16, 2007 3:30 am Probably that's going to be all... Maybe they wanna keep the tension up by not showing us nothing!!

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Jun 16, 2007 12:26 pm Trailers are made to advertise a movie and get more people to view it. Do they really need another trailer to get people to watch this?

[]:             Quote Post by Annihilator » Sat Jun 16, 2007 1:35 pm no, but showing people some clips from the movie would probably get some people more interested... and i would like to see one :P

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Sat Jun 16, 2007 2:40 pm The trailer they release is plenty. Created so much tension, that if they release footage the tension might lessen.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jun 16, 2007 3:01 pm I like to go into a movie not knowing what to expect. Sadly, that makes it kind of hard to find a movie you want to watch. It's great to have no expectations whatsoever. If Gainax didn't release any footage or information from the film, I would be all the happier.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jun 16, 2007 3:12 pm Most movie trailers are the best 2-3 minutes of the film, shuffled up, leaving no point in watching the rest. I just look to the write-up in the programme/on the website of our local art-house, like http://www.picturehouses.co.uk/cinema_home_date.aspx?eventId=1g6m4s%40camb

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Jun 16, 2007 4:11 pm EoE's trailer was a big scrambled mess of images from the film set to screaming noises.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Jun 17, 2007 12:41 am Well I figure they'll put out a cryptic trailer of some sort in mid-to-end July. Cause the first trailer was simply a trailer for these Evangelion movies as a WHOLE. There will be at least one inidivdual trailer for each movie. Never having seen too many Japanese anime trailers myself I don;t know what they'll look like but it makes sense to me. Evangelion does have a built in audience theatrically in Japan (teh U.S. not so much I think) and the fact that the film exists is advertising enough but I'm sure they'll want to try and gain some new fans (remember- this Eva is supposed to be accesible for the newbie as well) and to do that is to put out a trailer. I'm guessing within the next month. The American film business because of its excess of money (there's like 8 movies made a summer with a budget so high it could feed all of Africa for a year practically) is one of the few that allows excess marketing, hence why you can get trailers for movies 8 months in advance or so forth. It's still a little under 3 months til Eva 1.0 comes out so no rush. Most U.S. movies actually don;t get a FULL trailer til about a month and a half before release. My estimate for an Eva 1.0 (just so I can be proven wrong) is.... July 14-21

[]:             Quote Post by Typhus » Sun Jun 17, 2007 10:04 pm I'm happy they arent making a new one. LIke someone said earlyer trailers these days show practically all the movie. For example just look at the Spiderman3 trailer. They showed way to much of it. I could practicly put the whole movie togeather before I even saw it months before it came out.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Jun 17, 2007 10:37 pm Another good example is Borat. Most of the best moments were condensed down into the trailer. Watching the movie felt like watching a really long trailer with redundant jokes.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Jun 17, 2007 10:43 pm It's true that some of the best jokes were in the Borat trailer, but there was still tons of comedy gold to be found throughout the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Jun 17, 2007 11:30 pm BrikHaus wrote: It's true that some of the best jokes were in the Borat trailer, but there was still tons of dog poop to be found throughout the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Jun 18, 2007 2:00 am But the dog poop is all hidden... you usually know where to look for it in the background ahead of time.

[]:             Quote Post by Vampire Killer » Thu Jun 21, 2007 4:15 am Annihilator wrote: no, but showing people some clips from the movie would probably get some people more interested... and i would like to see one :P

[]:             Quote Post by Annihilator » Thu Jun 21, 2007 2:41 pm So am I.. They dont have to show a lot, just enought to get me more interested.

[]:             Quote Post by Hailtheplatypus » Thu Jun 21, 2007 5:59 pm Even some more developed concept art would suffice... I just want to see what direction they are taking this movie. I do not want to see a Hollywood style trailer that spoils the movie, just something concrete to show what the evas now look like...

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Thu Jun 21, 2007 7:08 pm Hailtheplatypus wrote: Even some more developed concept art would suffice... I just want to see what direction they are taking this movie. I do not want to see a Hollywood style trailer that spoils the movie, just something concrete to show what the evas now look like...

[]:             Quote Post by reagen » Sat Jun 16, 2007 3:30 am Probably that's going to be all... Maybe they wanna keep the tension u
